sutta: monks these 9 persons are found living in the world what 9 the arahant he who has entered on the path to arahanship the anagamin a non-returner he was entered on the path to the realization of the fruit of the non-returner the saka the kamin or 1's returner he was entered on the path to the realization of the fruit of the once returner the sotapanna or stream winner he who has entered on the path to the realization of the fruit of the stream winner and the ordinary man putujana amongst these 9 persons are found living in the world that's the end of this there's a few implications from this sutta the fact that these 9 persons are found living in the world is shows that path attainers are to be found in the world that's why in some other suta in the majima nikaya i think it is mentioned that you can make an offering to a path attainer so it is not that the path attainer exists for just 1 moment it does exist [Music] another implication that can be drawn from here you see here is mentioned the 8 uh aryans plus the puttujana that makes 9 persons and it is not mentioned here about the samasan buddha and the pachika buddha so what this meant here is that the sama sambuda the pachika buddha they are arahants in fact in the sutas you find that the buddha calls himself the arahant and that term arahan during the buddha's time was not used for his disciples the buddha referred to his enlightened disciples as aryan disciples aryan disciples who have who are liberated by wisdom or liberated by mind so the arahant includes the arahan disciple the samasan buddha and the pachaka buddha pasika buddha is that type of buddha who does not teach the dhamma to the world [Music] another thing you notice here is that the bodhisattva is not mentioned here and but the bodhisattva in our earliest early sutas is is either can be a putu jana or he can be a sota panna or he can be a sakadagamin stream winner or once return because bodhisatta comes back to the world so he must be 1 of these 3 an ordinary person a stream winner or a once returner and it's only the later books that say that the bodhisattva must be a pupujana but that is not seen in the sutas the other thing here is that the term sotapanna sakatakamin they refer to the fruition attainers fruition attainers not path attainers although the translation here uh in the in the in the books in the book
